events.text: Edward Moore, 76-year-old Other male, Never smoker. Lives independently. ECOG performance status 1.
Diagnosed with Acinar adenocarcinoma, Gleason 4+5=9 of the Prostate. Clinical staging: T3bN1M1b, AJCC Stage IV. ICD-10: C61. Stage IV castration-resistant prostate cancer with bone metastases.
PSMA PET/CT performed for baseline staging.
Pathology specimen received for initial biopsy.
Clinical visit for initial oncology consultation.
NGS report received for molecular profiling.
Initiated first-line ADT with ARPI: Leuprolide 22.5 mg IM q3mo (ADT) + Enzalutamide 160 mg daily. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for follow-up on first-line ADT with ARPI.
Clinical visit for follow-up on first-line ADT with ARPI.
Initiated first-line chemotherapy for CRPC: Docetaxel 75 mg/m2 q3w x 6 cycles + Prednisone 5 mg BID. Cycle 1. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Initiated first-line chemotherapy for CRPC: Docetaxel 75 mg/m2 q3w x 6 cycles + Prednisone 5 mg BID. Cycle 2. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 2 assessment.
Developed grade 1 hot flashes. Venlafaxine 37.5 mg daily started. Counseled on lifestyle modifications.
Initiated first-line chemotherapy for CRPC: Docetaxel 75 mg/m2 q3w x 6 cycles + Prednisone 5 mg BID. Cycle 3. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Initiated first-line chemotherapy for CRPC: Docetaxel 75 mg/m2 q3w x 6 cycles + Prednisone 5 mg BID. Cycle 4. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 4 assessment.
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast performed for restaging after first-line chemotherapy for CRPC.
Clinical visit for results review after first-line chemotherapy for CRPC.
Completed palliative bone radiation: Palliative radiation 30 Gy in 10 fractions to L3 vertebral body. Treatment delivered as planned.
Clinical visit for post-radiation assessment.
Initiated second-line PARP inhibitor for BRCA2-mutant CRPC: Olaparib 300 mg BID. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for follow-up on second-line PARP inhibitor for BRCA2-mutant CRPC.
Clinical visit for follow-up on second-line PARP inhibitor for BRCA2-mutant CRPC.
Bone scan performed for surveillance imaging.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
PSMA PET/CT performed for surveillance imaging.

documents.text:
PSMA PET/CT REPORT

Patient: Edward Moore
MRN: patient_3e470209859a
Date of Study: 2023-04-25

CLINICAL INDICATION: Acinar adenocarcinoma, Gleason 4+5=9 of the prostate. baseline staging.

COMPARISON: None available.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT performed from skull base to iliac crest following administration of 100 mL Omnipaque 350 IV contrast.

FINDINGS:
LUNGS: Clear. No pulmonary nodules or masses. No pleural effusion.

MEDIASTINUM: No significant lymphadenopathy. Heart size normal.

LIVER: Homogeneous attenuation. No focal lesions.

PANCREAS: Normal in size and attenuation. No pancreatic mass or ductal dilation.

KIDNEYS: Normal in size bilaterally. No hydronephrosis or renal masses.

PELVIS: Enlarged prostate measuring 4.9 cm. Bilateral pelvic lymphadenopathy, largest node 2.3 cm.

BONES: Multiple mixed lytic-sclerotic osseous lesions involving thoracic and lumbar spine consistent with osseous metastatic disease.

IMPRESSION:
1. Widespread osseous metastatic disease.
2. Pelvic lymphadenopathy consistent with nodal metastases.

Electronically signed by: David Kim, MD
Department of Radiology

PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient: Edward Moore
MRN: patient_3e470209859a
Specimen ID: S2023-39216
Date of Procedure: 2023-04-29
Date of Report: 2023-05-04

SPECIMEN: Prostate, excision/biopsy

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
Acinar adenocarcinoma, Gleason 4+5=9, well differentiated.
Greatest tumor dimension: 1.8 cm
Margins: Positive at inked margin
Lymphovascular invasion: Present
Perineural invasion: Not identified

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
PSA: Positive
NKX3.1: Positive
AR: Positive
ERG: Positive (TMPRSS2-ERG fusion likely)

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Edward Moore - Prostate" is a tan-white tissue fragment measuring 1.8 x 1.3 x 0.9 cm. The cut surface reveals a soft tan mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A6.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a well differentiated neoplasm composed of cords and trabeculae of atypical cells with scant cytoplasm and prominent nucleoli. Mitotic figures are identified (6 per 10 HPF). Focal necrosis is present. The surrounding stroma shows desmoplastic reaction.

Electronically signed by: Patricia Williams, MD
Department of Pathology

INITIAL CONSULTATION - Medical Oncology

Patient: Edward Moore
MRN: patient_3e470209859a
Date: 2023-05-02
Provider: Dr. Amanda Foster

CHIEF COMPLAINT: initial oncology consultation

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Mr./Ms. Moore is a 76-year-old male who presents for initial evaluation of newly diagnosed acinar adenocarcinoma, gleason 4+5=9 of the prostate, IV. Patient presented with symptoms prompting workup. Stage IV castration-resistant prostate cancer with bone metastases. Pathology confirmed acinar adenocarcinoma, gleason 4+5=9. Molecular testing is pending. Patient reports fatigue and unintentional 10-lb weight loss over 3 months.

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY:
- obstructive sleep apnea
- obesity

SURGICAL HISTORY:
- Appendectomy (age 25)

SOCIAL HISTORY:
- Current smoker (1 ppd)
- Alcohol: Rare alcohol use
- Occupation: Accountant
- Lives with partner

FAMILY HISTORY:
- No family history of cancer.

ALLERGIES: NKDA

MEDICATIONS:
- None

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
Positive for: mild cough, mild peripheral edema, intermittent headaches, joint pain. Remainder of 14-point ROS reviewed and negative.

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
Vitals: BP 143/89, HR 69, RR 14, Temp 97.9F, SpO2 99% on RA, Wt 93.5 kg
General: Comfortable, thin-appearing, no acute distress.
HEENT: No scleral icterus. Oropharynx clear.
Neck: No thyromegaly. No lymphadenopathy.
Lungs: Clear bilaterally. No rales or rhonchi.
CV: RRR, S1/S2 normal. No murmurs, gallops, or rubs.
Abdomen: Soft, non-distended. Well-healed midline surgical scar.
Extremities: No peripheral edema. Good pulses bilaterally.
Neuro: Alert and oriented. No focal neurological deficits.

LABORATORY RESULTS:
WBC 4.6, Hgb 14.6, Plt 281, Cr 1.05

IMAGING:
See separate imaging report.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
76-year-old male with IV acinar adenocarcinoma, gleason 4+5=9 of the prostate. initial oncology consultation.

1. Acinar adenocarcinoma, Gleason 4+5=9 of the prostate, Stage IV
   - Reviewed pathology and imaging with patient and family
   - Recommend first-line ADT with ARPI
   - Will discuss at multidisciplinary tumor board
   - Molecular profiling results pending
2. Supportive care
   - Nutrition consultation placed
   - Social work referral for psychosocial support
   - Peripheral access adequate
3. Follow-up in 1-2 weeks to initiate treatment

Electronically signed by: Amanda Foster, MD
Medical Oncology

NEXT GENERATION SEQUENCING REPORT

Patient: Edward Moore
MRN: patient_3e470209859a
Date of Report: 2023-05-06
Specimen: Prostate (FNA cell block)
Diagnosis: Acinar adenocarcinoma, Gleason 4+5=9
Panel: MSK-IMPACT (648 genes)

RESULTS SUMMARY:
Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB): 2.6 mutations/Mb (Low)
Microsatellite Status: MSS

CLINICALLY SIGNIFICANT GENOMIC ALTERATIONS:
  BRCA2 p.E1493fs (c.4478delA) - Frameshift (Exon 11) [Clinically Significant]

ADDITIONAL PATHOGENIC ALTERATIONS:
  TP53 p.R273C - Missense (Exon 8)
  PTEN Loss - Homozygous deletion

VARIANTS OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE:
  ATM p.S1893R - VUS
  CDK12 p.Q1142H - VUS

COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS:
  No significant copy number alterations detected

FUSIONS:
  No pathogenic fusions detected.

THERAPY ASSOCIATIONS:
  - BRCA2 pathogenic variant: FDA-approved PARP inhibitor olaparib indicated for HRR-mutated mCRPC.
  - Consider platinum-based chemotherapy.

Note: This test was performed using hybrid capture-based next generation sequencing.
Sequencing depth: mean 555x coverage.
Tumor content: 53%.

Electronically signed by: Katherine Liu, MD, PhD
Molecular Pathology Laboratory

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Mr./Ms. Moore is a 76-year-old male with IV acinar adenocarcinoma, gleason 4+5=9 of the prostate who presents for follow-up on first-line adt with arpi. Patient reports mild fatigue but otherwise doing well. Appetite has improved.

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
Positive for: insomnia, intermittent nausea, abdominal bloating, mild peripheral edema. Remainder of 14-point ROS reviewed and negative.

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
Vitals: BP 131/79, HR 68, RR 19, Temp 98.4F, SpO2 98% on RA, Wt 99.4 kg
General: Comfortable, thin-appearing, no acute distress.
HEENT: NCAT, EOMI, PERRL. Oral mucosa moist without lesions.
Neck: Supple, no JVD, no cervical lymphadenopathy.
Lungs: Clear bilaterally. No rales or rhonchi.
CV: RRR, S1/S2 normal. No murmurs, gallops, or rubs.
Abdomen: Soft, mildly tender in RUQ. No rebound. Normoactive bowel sounds.
Extremities: Trace bilateral lower extremity edema. Warm and well-perfused.
Neuro: Alert and oriented. No focal neurological deficits.

LABORATORY RESULTS:
WBC 6.7, Hgb 13.2, Plt 206, Cr 0.9

IMAGING:
Most recent imaging reviewed.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
76-year-old male with IV acinar adenocarcinoma, gleason 4+5=9 of the prostate. follow-up on first-line ADT with ARPI.

1. Acinar adenocarcinoma, Gleason 4+5=9 - follow-up on first-line ADT with ARPI
   - Proceed with next cycle as planned

2. Symptom management

3. Follow-up in 6 weeks

BONE SCAN REPORT

Patient: Edward Moore
MRN: patient_3e470209859a
Date of Study: 2024-06-28

CLINICAL INDICATION: Acinar adenocarcinoma, Gleason 4+5=9 of the prostate. surveillance imaging.

COMPARISON: Prior study dated approximately 6 weeks ago available for comparison.

TECHNIQUE: Whole body bone scintigraphy performed following IV injection of 25 mCi Tc-99m MDP.

FINDINGS:
Multiple foci of increased radiotracer uptake in the axial and appendicular skeleton consistent with widespread osseous metastatic disease. Approximately 14 lesions identified.

IMPRESSION:
1. Widespread osseous metastatic disease.
2. Pelvic lymphadenopathy consistent with nodal metastases.
3. Overall assessment: Stable disease per RECIST 1.1.

Electronically signed by: David Kim, MD
Department of Radiology
